name: Right Clavicle
description: S-shaped strut connecting the upper limb to the axial skeleton. Articulates medially with the sternum and laterally with the acromion of the scapula.
classification: Long bone (the only horizontal long bone in the body)
articulations: Manubrium of the sternum — sternoclavicular joint
Acromion of the scapula — acromioclavicular joint
muscleAttachments: Pectoralis major — anterior border, medial third
clinical: The clavicle is the most commonly fractured bone in the body.
Fractures usually occur at the junction of its two curves (middle third), often from a fall on an outstretched hand or the shoulder.
